stories.headline: OpenAI and Broadcom Unveil 'Jalapeño,' OpenAI's First Custom AI Inference Chip
stories.key_takeaway: OpenAI and Broadcom have unveiled Jalapeño, OpenAI's first custom inference chip, a reticle-sized ASIC built in a record nine months that the companies say beats current state-of-the-art performance per watt and ships at gigawatt scale in late 2026.

pull_quote.text: The world is moving to a compute-powered economy. Jalapeño is part of our long-term full-stack infrastructure strategy to make compute more abundant, resulting in AI which is faster, more reliable, more affordable for people and businesses, and can be used to solve more important problems.

data_points.label: Design-to-tape-out cycle, claimed fastest ever for a high-performance ASIC
Start of large-scale deployment at gigawatt scale
Share of first batch Microsoft is asked to commit to
Estimated die size, near the EUV reticle limit

stories.article_excerpt: OpenAI stepped onto the silicon stage this week, unveiling Jalapeño, its first custom AI chip, built with Broadcom.

stories.headline: Google Invests $75 Million in A24 for a DeepMind AI Filmmaking Partnership
stories.key_takeaway: Google has taken its first-ever equity stake in a Hollywood studio, investing roughly $75 million in A24 for a non-exclusive, multi-year DeepMind research partnership focused on AI filmmaking tools, even as A24's own filmmakers and fans revolt.

pull_quote.text: This partnership exists because we want to dictate what tools get built for artists, and so they have a voice in shaping them rather than having tools handed to them. We'd rather have a seat at the table than on the sidelines.

data_points.label: Google's investment in A24
Google's first-ever stake in a film studio
Non-exclusive term of the partnership
The first AI tool targeted

stories.article_excerpt: Google has invested roughly $75 million in A24 for a DeepMind AI filmmaking partnership, sparking a fierce backlash.

stories.headline: Taktile Raises $110 Million From Goldman Sachs to Automate High-Stakes Financial Decisions
stories.key_takeaway: Taktile has raised a $110 million Series C led by Growth Equity at Goldman Sachs Alternatives, bringing total funding to $184 million, as banks and insurers move from AI pilots to automating high-stakes underwriting, claims and fraud decisions.

pull_quote.text: Today, thousands of employees process these decisions manually. Leaders want to redeploy that capacity to higher-value work, while ensuring every outcome — whether human or AI-driven — remains the best for the business and customers. This is what Taktile enables.

data_points.label: Series C raised, led by Goldman Sachs Alternatives
Total capital raised to date
Automation achieved in B2B underwriting
Reduction in anti-money-laundering false positives

stories.article_excerpt: Taktile lands a $110M Series C from Goldman Sachs to push AI into banks' and insurers' highest-stakes decisions.

stories.headline: Assort Health Raises $120 Million to Scale AI Agents Across the Patient Journey
stories.key_takeaway: Assort Health raised a $120 million Series C led by Menlo Ventures at a $1.2 billion valuation to expand its AI agent platform from voice scheduling into a full system of record for the patient journey.

pull_quote.text: Every so often a company comes along that fundamentally reimagines how an industry operates. Assort is that company for healthcare. They've built not just another point solution, but a unified platform of AI agents that elevates the entire patient journey.

data_points.label: Series C raised
Post-money valuation
Total capital raised to date
Revenue growth over 15 months

stories.article_excerpt: Assort Health raised a $120M Series C led by Menlo Ventures at a $1.2B valuation to scale its AI agent platform.

stories.headline: Anthropic Projects Its First-Ever Operating Profit of $559 Million as Run Rate Hits $47 Billion
stories.key_takeaway: Anthropic told investors it expects its first operating profit of $559 million on $10.9 billion in Q2 revenue, but the figure is an unaudited internal projection that critics say leans on temporarily discounted compute costs.

pull_quote.text: Anthropic's 'profitability' is a result of accountancy rather than any improvements to its business model.
pull_quote.role: Technology writer, Where's Your Ed At

data_points.label: Projected Q2 2026 operating profit (first ever)
Projected Q2 revenue, up 130% from Q1
Annualized revenue run rate in May
Compute cost per revenue dollar in Q2, down from 71¢

stories.article_excerpt: Anthropic told investors it expects a $559M operating profit on $10.9B in Q2 revenue — but the numbers are unaudited.

stories.headline: Mistral Releases OCR 4, a Single-Container Document Intelligence Tool Spanning 170 Languages
stories.key_takeaway: Mistral's OCR 4 turns document extraction into structured, citation-ready intelligence across 170 languages and can run entirely inside a customer's own infrastructure as a single container, a pitch aimed at regulated enterprises wary of US cloud dependency.

pull_quote.text: We benchmarked Mistral OCR 4 against the leading agentic document parsers across a chart and figure dense financial QA dataset and reached equivalent accuracy at roughly 8x lower cost and 17x lower latency. For production use cases at scale, that delta compounds fast.

data_points.label: Languages supported, across 10 language groups
API price ($2 with Batch-API discount)
Top overall score on OlmOCRBench
Average win rate in blind human evaluations

stories.article_excerpt: Mistral's OCR 4 brings structured, citation-ready document AI to 170 languages, deployable as a single self-hosted container.

stories.headline: Sakana AI Releases Fugu Ultra, an Orchestration Model That Commands Other LLMs
stories.key_takeaway: Sakana AI's Fugu Ultra is not a monolithic LLM but the flagship tier of Sakana Fugu, a multi-agent orchestration model that routes tasks across a swappable pool of frontier LLMs behind a single API.

pull_quote.text: Relying on a single company's APIs for critical infrastructure, finance, or governance is a material vulnerability. This risk is no longer a hypothetical possibility, but a reality.

data_points.label: Fugu Ultra score on SWE-Bench Pro (vs 69.2 for Opus 4.8)
Benchmarks where a Fugu model posts the top score
Early users in the Fugu closed beta
Fugu Ultra price per million input / output tokens

stories.article_excerpt: Sakana AI's Fugu Ultra isn't a bigger model — it's a model that commands other models, behind one API.

stories.headline: Kuaishou Ships KLING v3.0 and v3.0 Turbo, Raising the Bar for AI Video
stories.key_takeaway: Kuaishou's KLING 3.0 family — anchored by the new, audio-included KLING 3.0 Turbo shipped June 17 — pushes native 4K, multi-shot storyboarding and cheaper per-second pricing, underscoring Chinese labs' fast-rising momentum in AI video.

pull_quote.role: Kling AI team, describing 3.0's character-consistency engine

data_points.label: Native KLING 3.0 output resolution and frame rate
KLING 3.0 Turbo list price at 720p, audio included
Max clip length and camera cuts per generation
KLING 3.0's reported leaderboard score at launch

stories.article_excerpt: Kuaishou's KLING 3.0 Turbo brings audio-included, native-4K AI video at cheaper per-second rates as Chinese labs surge.

stories.headline: NVIDIA Ships GR00T-H-N1.7, a Commercial Foundation Model for Surgical Robotics
stories.key_takeaway: NVIDIA has released GR00T-H-N1.7, the first commercially licensed foundation model for surgical robotics, giving developers a shared, fine-tunable starting point — though it remains explicitly off-limits for patient care.

pull_quote.text: By responsibly contributing surgical data and training open models on NVIDIA's physical AI platform, medical technology leaders are accelerating a new generation of intelligent robotic systems that can assist surgeons, scale surgical expertise and ultimately expand access to high-quality care.
pull_quote.role: Head of Healthcare Business Development, NVIDIA

data_points.label: Parameters in the GR00T-H-N1.7 model
Open-H-Embodiment dataset, 20 platforms, 50+ institutions
Anonymized Versius data from CMR Surgical
End-to-end suturing success on SutureBot (a baseline)

stories.article_excerpt: NVIDIA's GR00T-H-N1.7 gives surgical robotics a shared, commercially licensed foundation model — but not a path into the OR yet.

stories.headline: New Model Forecasts Scientific Breakthroughs From How Concept Networks Evolve
stories.key_takeaway: A Swiss research team built an interpretable model that predicts which pairs of scientific concepts are about to fuse into breakthroughs, hitting ROC-AUC of 0.95-0.97 while keeping every prediction traceable to auditable network features.

pull_quote.text: Feature attribution shows that structural factors — particularly Adamic-Adar similarity and degree-based Hadamard measures — consistently drive accuracy, suggesting that breakthrough-relevant recombinations emerge in tightly connected sub-networks.

data_points.label: ROC-AUC across four domains, vs ~0.90 prior models
Semantic and topological features in the model
RMSLE for link-intensity prediction over 1-5 years
Expert-anchored quantum case studies

stories.article_excerpt: A new interpretable model predicts scientific breakthroughs by watching how concept networks rewire themselves over time.

stories.headline: Content-Adaptive Sparse Attention Cuts Long-Context Compute by 10x or More, With Near-Lossless Quality
stories.key_takeaway: Two verified 2026 results — MiniMax's MSA (shipped in a 109B production model) and the DHSA framework (ICML 2026 Spotlight) — show content-adaptive sparsity cutting long-context attention compute by roughly 10x to 28x while staying near-lossless.

pull_quote.text: On a 109B-parameter model with native multimodal training, MSA performs on par with GQA while reducing per-token attention compute by 28.4x at 1M context.

data_points.label: Per-token attention compute reduction at 1M context (MSA)
Prefill and decoding wall-clock speedups on H800
Prefill speedup at 128K context for DHSA
DHSA accuracy gain over Block Sparse Attention

stories.article_excerpt: A wave of June 2026 research makes attention content-adaptive, slashing long-context compute roughly 10x while staying near-lossless.

stories.headline: Penn Researchers Build a Light-Matter Particle to Speed Up AI Computing at Lower Energy
stories.key_takeaway: Penn physicists used exciton-polaritons, hybrid light-matter quasiparticles, to demonstrate all-optical switching at roughly 4 femtojoules per operation, a step toward photonic AI hardware that computes without converting light back to electricity.

pull_quote.text: Because they are charge-neutral and have zero rest mass, photons can carry information quickly over long distances with minimal loss. But that neutrality means they barely interact with their environment, making them bad at the sort of signal-switching logic that computers depend on.
pull_quote.role: Co-first author; Assistant Professor, Montana State University

data_points.label: Energy per all-light switching operation
Atomically thin semiconductor hosting the polaritons
Published in Physical Review Letters
Funded by US Office of Naval Research and Sloan Foundation

stories.article_excerpt: Penn physicists made a hybrid light-matter particle switch light with light at femtojoule energies, a possible path past AI's power wall.

stories.headline: US Government Asks OpenAI to Stagger GPT-5.6 Rollout to 'Trusted Partners' in an Unprecedented Move
stories.key_takeaway: For the first time, the US government preemptively asked an American AI company to restrict a frontier model launch, with OpenAI agreeing to limit GPT-5.6 to government-vetted partners while warning the arrangement should not become permanent.

pull_quote.text: We don't believe this kind of government access process should become the long-term default. It keeps the best tools from users, developers, enterprises, cyber defenders, and global partners who need them.

data_points.label: New GPT-5.6 models released (Sol, Terra, Luna)
Sol's score on OpenAI's internal cyber CTF test
'Trusted partners' getting early access
Pre-release review window under the June 2 executive order

stories.article_excerpt: Washington for the first time preemptively asked a US AI lab to gate a frontier launch, and OpenAI said yes — reluctantly.

stories.headline: Anthropic Accuses Alibaba of Illicitly Accessing Claude With 25,000 Fake Accounts
stories.key_takeaway: Anthropic has told U.S. lawmakers that operators linked to Alibaba's Qwen lab used roughly 25,000 fake accounts to run 28.8 million Claude queries in what it calls its largest-ever model-distillation attack, even as Anthropic fights its own US export curbs.

pull_quote.text: We disagree that the finding of a narrow potential jailbreak should be cause for recalling a commercial model deployed to hundreds of millions of people.

data_points.label: Fraudulent accounts allegedly used
Claude interactions generated
Window of the alleged 2026 campaign
Prior Chinese-lab campaigns Anthropic disclosed

stories.article_excerpt: Anthropic has accused Alibaba-linked operators of using 25,000 fake accounts to siphon Claude's most valuable capabilities.
